title: SACRAMENTO, Calif --- Today, Assemblymember Mike A. Gipson (D-Carson) released the following statement regarding his budget proposal to support foster youth:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Too many vulnerable foster youth have been arrested by law enforcement for low-level offenses.",
    "Children of color, girls, LGBTQ youth, and youth with disabilities are disproportionately represented among foster youth arrested for low-level offenses.",
    "In the 2016 annual report on dual status youth in Los Angeles, almost 40% of foster youth who crossed over into the delinquency system were residing in group care at the time of referral.",
    "The pipeline from foster youth to the justice system is considered unacceptable.",
    "The proposed budget allocation is $7,575,000 million every year for three years to support community-based programs for foster youth.",
    "Community-based organizations will provide trauma-informed training to group home shelter staff and law enforcement as part of the budget proposal.",
    "Access to cultural centers, arts programs, and extracurricular activities can positively impact the social and emotional development of foster youth.",
    "Civic engagement, mentorship, and mental health mindfulness can transform how foster youth view themselves and their futures."
  ]
}
```
